heading: Pyth-2 page-133 ratio/proportion layer stabilizes as one continuous bead chain, not multiple answer families
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, ratio-proportion, bead-chain, cattle-problem]
body: Built the page-133 ratio/proportion alternative as a tested lane instead of leaving it as prose. `pyth2_ratio_proportion_bead_chain.py` now reconstructs the four fraction-derived Fibonacci bead quadruples from equations (4) through (7): `2/7, 1/42, 13/42, 1/3`; `1/3, 1/30, 11/30, 2/5`; `2/5, 1/20, 9/20, 1/2`; and `1/2, 1/12, 7/12, 2/3`. Each row satisfies the Fibonacci-type rule, adjacent rows overlap exactly at `1/3`, `2/5`, and `1/2`, and the whole lane integerizes cleanly at common scale `420` to `[120,10,130,140]`, `[140,14,154,168]`, `[168,21,189,210]`, and `[210,35,245,280]`. Honest verdict: this is a real ratio/proportion construction lane and it supports a coupled bead-derivation reading of the cattle fractions rather than multiple unrelated answer families, but it still does not extract explicit cattle counts by itself. The post-cows lane now has 7 stable items and 3 unresolved items.
